problem: The figure shows a dotted grid $8$ cells wide and $3$ cells tall, consisting of $1'' \times 1''$ squares. Carl places $1$-inch toothpicks along some sides of the squares to create a closed loop that does not intersect itself. The numbers in cells indicate the number of sides covered; any number of toothpicks are allowed if no number is written. In how many ways can Carl place the toothpicks?
